blocking_gaps: Add schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys == Config ApplyMap keys (drift guard) — currently only html/json have this; it is an L4 requirement and the only Config-dimension gap (core/formats/androidxml/schema.go vs config.go).

Add an encoding/line-ending edge-case matrix: fixtures + tests for UTF-16/BOM-prefixed and CRLF strings.xml proving the splice writer round-trips them byte-faithfully (no test in core/formats/androidxml/*_test.go references bom/utf-16/crlf; the reader uses unsafe.String over io.ReadAll bytes and never inspects encoding/BOM).

Eliminate any native-bug xfails (vacuously satisfied — no parity table — but L4 also wants fresh contract-audit/parity dashboards, which do not apply to a harvest format; document this as the permanent ceiling per the harvest open-question).
formats.top_risk: The reader publishes the entire original document on the Layer via unsafe.String aliasing the io.ReadAll buffer (reader.go:145), and the writer re-tokenizes those exact bytes to splice. Correctness of every byte-faithful round-trip depends on nothing mutating that backing array or the layer property between read and write; the comment asserts content is not mutated, but it is an unguarded invariant — any upstream tool that edited Layer.Properties["androidxml.original"] or the shared slice would silently corrupt output. Plus an encoding blind spot: the writer assumes UTF-8/byte-level splicing and is untested against UTF-16/BOM inputs, where byte-faithful splicing of entity-encoded ASCII replacements into a UTF-16 stream would produce malformed output.

blocking_gaps: Add a schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys == ApplyMap-accepted keys (extractComments, protectPlaceholders) and defaults match the Config struct -- this is the named L4 requirement and the only thing keeping Config at 'partial' (cite core/formats/applestrings/schema.go vs config.go ApplyMap).

Add an encoding/line-ending edge-case MATRIX fixture set: currently only UTF-16LE+BOM byte-round-trip is proven (TestUTF16RoundTrip in roundtrip_test.go). Add UTF-16BE round-trip, CRLF-line-ending .strings, BOM-bearing UTF-8, and an all-Unicode/emoji-in-key fixture, each asserting byte-faithful round-trip (the L3->L4 'edge-case matrix' criterion).

Promote the gated acceptance_test.go (plutil -lint, //go:build acceptance, skips off-macOS) into the routinely-run matrix or document its CI execution, so writer spec-compliance is continuously proven rather than opt-in.
formats.top_risk: UTF-16 re-encode of an EDITED value can be lossy: encodeUTF16 goes through []rune+utf16.Encode (unpaired surrogates/invalid sequences become U+FFFD), and the BOM-less UTF-16 detection heuristic (NUL-in-odd-position count over first 256 bytes) can misclassify short or non-ASCII-heavy files. Untouched files are safe via stored-original splice, but a translated UTF-16 .strings with astral-plane chars in a changed value risks corruption, and no edge-case test covers it (only one ASCII UTF-16LE fixture exists).

blocking_gaps: schema_test.go: add a test asserting Schema() property keys == ApplyMap-accepted keys (descriptionNotes) so the schema cannot drift from the Config struct — the explicit L4 schema gate (only html/json have this today). Files: add core/formats/arb/schema_test.go against schema.go + config.go.

Encoding/BOM/CRLF/UTF-16 edge-case fixture matrix: all current fixtures (simple_en, icu_en, translated_fr, compact_en, corpus/*) are 2-space LF UTF-8. Add fixtures with a UTF-8 BOM, CRLF line endings, and an all-Unicode message, and assert byte-faithful round-trip — the L4 'edge-case matrix' requirement. Files: core/formats/arb/testdata/ + roundtrip_test.go.
formats.top_risk: Byte-faithful round-trip depends on the writer's hand-rolled JSON scanner/rewriter (scanner.go + rewrite.go + encode.go) staying in lockstep with the reader's parser (parse.go). If a value is edited, the changed string is re-emitted via encodeJSONString (the writer's own escaper), whose escaping conventions must exactly match the input file's — yet there is no test that EDITS a value containing characters with multiple valid JSON encodings (e.g. a forward slash, a non-ASCII char that gen_l10n leaves raw, or a control char) and asserts the re-encoded form is what Flutter gen_l10n would emit. The acceptance test only checks the output is schema-valid JSON, not encoding-faithful. A mismatch would silently produce semantically-correct but byte-divergent (or over-escaped) output on edited messages — invisible to the current corpus tests, which only round-trip UNCHANGED values byte-for-byte.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/csv/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated/garbage input (unbalanced quotes spanning lines, lone CR, binary/non-CSV bytes, oversized field) and run under -race; this is the L2-level criterion CSV currently fails (rubric line 25) and the L4 robustness blocker the published snapshot cites.

Cover the inline-code path: a reader_test that sets useCodeFinder+codeFinderRules and asserts the resulting block emits Ph/PlaceholderRun runs with verbatim Data (Reader dimension; applyCodeFinder in reader.go:668-718 is entirely untested).

Promote the default (non-skeleton) writer round-trip assertions from Contains(...) to byte/semantic equality, or document why the csv.NewWriter reconstruction path's normalization is spec-justified (Writer fidelity; register default uses NewWriter with no skeleton store).
formats.top_risk: The registered default CSV writer (NewWriter, no skeleton store) reconstructs output via Go's csv.NewWriter, which re-quotes and normalizes fields and rebuilds rows from col/row block properties — it is NOT byte-faithful and its only tests assert Contains(...) rather than equality. Byte-exactness is proven solely for the skeleton path. So an ordinary extract->translate->merge that does not wire a skeleton store can silently alter quoting/whitespace/empty-cell layout, and no test would catch it; combined with the total absence of malformed-input panic/error coverage, broken or quote-spanning CSV is the largest untested correctness/robustness surface."

blocking_gaps: Corpus breadth (medium): the only real-world corpus file (style-dictionary-demo) has NO $description, so the real-file corpus exercises only the empty-translatable-surface path. Vendor 1-2 upstream DTCG files that actually carry $description (e.g. Tokens Studio / a documented design system) and assert byte-faithful translate→write on them in core/formats/designtokens/corpus_test.go.

Encoding / BOM / CRLF / Unicode edge-case matrix (L3→L4, medium): no fixtures for non-UTF-8, UTF-8 BOM, CRLF line endings, or all-Unicode $description. Add an edge-case matrix in designtokens_test.go proving byte-faithful round-trip across these (the rubric's stated L4 gap for this format).

schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys == ApplyMap keys (high weight for the Config dimension at L4): only html/json have this drift guard today. Add core/formats/designtokens/schema_test.go so a new ApplyMap key can never silently diverge from schema.go (currently both list only extractDescriptions, but nothing pins them together).
formats.top_risk: Translation-path byte-fidelity is proven almost entirely on the SYNTHETIC in-package fixture (tokens.tokens.json): the one real upstream corpus file carries no $description, so the translate→splice→write path against genuine, messily-formatted real-world DTCG (irregular indentation/whitespace, escaped slashes, mixed key ordering) is never exercised byte-for-byte. A JSON-delegation splice bug that only manifests on real token tooling output (e.g. Tokens Studio's formatting) would slip past the green corpus suite. The strip-and-compare invariant proves semantic preservation but not byte preservation on real translated files.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (HIGH/critical, L1->L2 blocker): assert NotPanics + clean PartResult.Error on truncated comment blocks, garbage bytes, unterminated /** ... and /*! ..., invalid UTF-8; run under -race. core/formats/doxygen/ has none.
Add schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys == Config.ApplyMap accepted keys to close config/schema drift (Config dimension partial; only html/json have this today).
Add a dedicated corpus_test.go / upstream_test.go over vendored Okapi upstream doxygen fixtures (not just the 4 synthetic testdata/*.h) proving byte-exact roundtrip + stable counts (Corpus partial).
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness test: the reader is a hand-rolled line-by-line scanner (parseBlockComment, parseLineComments, processInlineCommands, extractDescAndPrefix, command-arg counting) operating on arbitrary source files, with zero coverage for truncated/unterminated comments, garbage bytes, or invalid UTF-8 — so a panic on a real-world malformed input would surface as an uncaught crash rather than a clean PartResult.Error, and nothing runs these paths under -race.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/dtd/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + clean PartResult.Error (or empty-block result) on truncated <!ENTITY, garbage bytes, unterminated quote/comment, and nil doc; run with -race. This is the primary L1->L2 blocker (45/48 systemic gap).

Add core/formats/dtd/schema.go (Config implementing SchemaProvider exposing useCodeFinder + codeFinderRules) so CLI/UI/reference can describe the knobs; ideally a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys. Required for L2 'schema.go present'.

Reconcile spec.yaml with Config: delete the stale 'no configurable knobs / ApplyMap rejects any input' header claim, enumerate useCodeFinder + codeFinderRules with okapi_param mappings, making spec keys 1:1 with ApplyMap (and update the nativedocs sidecar's matching claim). Required for the L2 spec criterion.
formats.top_risk: spec.yaml documents the native reader as having 'no configurable knobs (ApplyMap rejects any input),' but Config.ApplyMap actually accepts useCodeFinder + codeFinderRules. Okapi's DTDFilter defaults useCodeFinder=true with the HTML-tag rule, whereas the native reader defaults it OFF and the spec omits both params entirely — an unspecified default mismatch the spec hides rather than asserts. Under the bridge's default (code-finder on) vs native default (off), entity values containing raw markup are split into inline codes on one side but not the other, so parity is only 'passing' because the spec sidesteps the very config that controls it.

formats.okapi_counterpart: okf_epub (EpubFilter; exists upstream in Okapi okapi/filters/epub/ but NOT shipped in the okapi-bridge runtime manifest — verified absent from dist/plugin/manifest.json's 54 filters)
blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (HIGH weight; the L1->L2 blocker per the maturity report): assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on (a) corrupt/truncated ZIP bytes, (b) missing META-INF/container.xml, (c) container.xml with no rootfile, (d) rootfile full-path pointing at an absent OPF, (e) malformed OPF XML; run with -race. The error paths already exist in findOPF/parseOPF/zip.OpenReader emitError but nothing exercises them.

Add schema.go (HIGH weight; L2 Config+Schema requirement): even for a zero-parameter format, provide a Schema() so CLI/UI/reference docs and a schema_test (Schema keys == ApplyMap keys, here both empty) can assert no drift. Currently Config is a bare struct with no schema surface.

Promote round-trip tests to prove byte/semantic EQUALITY of untouched entries against the source, not 'output Contains text' (HIGH/critical weight, needed before L3/L4 Writer credit): TestSourcePath only compares two writer outputs to each other; add an assertion that skeleton-path output re-extracts byte-identical untouched ZIP entries (mimetype, CSS, images) from the original.
formats.top_risk: Writer data-loss in the no-skeleton writeEPUB fallback: replaceXHTMLText builds a map[sourceText]targetText, so two paragraphs with identical source text collapse to a single replacement key — duplicate or repeated strings in a chapter get the wrong (or only one) translation. Compounding this, that path re-serializes XHTML through xml.Encoder rather than preserving original bytes (attribute order, self-closing/entity forms, whitespace all drift), so 'untouched' content is silently rewritten. No test asserts byte/semantic equality of the source XHTML, so this corruption is currently undetected; only the skeleton path (which the tests mostly exercise via SetSkeletonStore) is faithful."

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (HIGH weight, L1->L2 blocker): assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated/garbage/binary/all-CR input and confirm nil-doc rejection, run under -race. core/formats/fixedwidth/ has none.

Add schema.go (HIGH weight, L2 Config requirement): a DataFormatConfig schema describing columns/hasHeader/trimValues for CLI/UI/reference; ideally a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys. Currently MISSING (config.go has the struct but no schema descriptor).
formats.top_risk: The non-skeleton writer path (writer.flush(), taken whenever no SkeletonStore is set) is silently lossy: it rebuilds each row from a blank space-padded buffer of computed lineWidth and overlays only collected cells, dropping inter-column gaps/filler, original line endings, and any layout outside declared columns, and truncating over-width translated values to the column width without error. Byte-faithfulness is proven only on the skeleton path the tests use; a caller that omits the skeleton store gets corrupted output, not an error.

blocking_gaps: Config (L1 floor, critical): ApplyMap silently ignores unknown keys — both the top-level switch in config.go (line ~111 `default: // Ignore unknown parameters`) and applyParserSettings (line ~127) need a default branch returning an 'unknown parameter' error so typos/invalid config are rejected. This is an explicit L1 criterion currently unmet.

Malformed (L2 floor, high): add core/formats/html/malformed_test.go feeding truncated/garbage/unclosed/binary input through reader.Open+Read, asserting NotPanics and either a clean PartResult.Error or well-defined lenient parse — run with -race. Currently only nil-Open is covered.
formats.top_risk: ApplyMap accepts unknown configuration keys silently (config.go default branches under both the top-level switch and applyParserSettings). A misspelled or stale config key (e.g. `preserveWhiteSpace`, `codeFinder`) is dropped with no error, so the format silently runs with default behavior instead of the user's intended extraction rules — a correctness footgun that also breaks the rubric's L1 'Config rejects unknown keys' contract and can mask config drift against the okf_html bridge.

blocking_gaps: Add schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys == Config ApplyMap-accepted keys (and types/defaults match the struct). Highest L3->L4 weight (Config/Schema=high); no such test exists today and the live drift below is exactly what it would catch.

Fix the nativedocs sidecar parameter-name drift in scripts/gen-refs/nativedocs/formats/i18next.yaml: 'subfilterHTMLValues' -> 'subfilterHtmlValues' in both the parameters block and the example config; add the missing protectInterpolation and legacyPluralForms parameters so the sidecar mirrors schema.go 1:1 (Docs=medium; required for L4 'no drift').

Add an encoding/CRLF/BOM/all-Unicode edge-case fixture matrix for byte-faithful round-trip (Writer=critical for L4). Current corpus + synthetic fixtures are all UTF-8 LF; L4 requires the edge-case matrix proven byte-exact.
formats.top_risk: Reader/writer correctness fully rides on the inner core/formats/json delegation: byte-faithful round-trip depends on the JSON reader replaying original token bytes for untouched values and on flattenInlineCodes re-splicing protected {{interpolation}}/$t() runs before write. Any JSON-layer change to tokenization, key-path naming (json.keypath/UseFullKeyPath), or escapeForwardSlashes silently changes i18next output. blockKeyPath also depends on the json.keypath property name; if the JSON reader renames it, plural/context annotation silently degrades to the slash-name fallback. The corpus byte-equal tests are the only guardrail catching such drift, and they are all UTF-8/LF — encoding/BOM/CRLF round-trip is unproven."

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/icml/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated/garbage/non-XML/nil input, run with -race. This is the single hardest L1->L2 blocker (highest rubric weight: Malformed is 'high'). Note the real defect it must expose: reader.go swallows xml.Decoder errors with a bare 'break' (lines 146-148 and 288-291) so malformed XML currently produces NO error on the channel — fix the reader to surface decode failures via emitError, then assert it.

Make spec.yaml config keys 1:1 with config.go ApplyMap (L2 Spec criterion, weight 'high'). Today spec declares extraction/extractMasterSpreads/simplifyCodes/skipThreshold/inlineCodes which ApplyMap rejects as 'unknown parameter'. Either implement+accept them in ApplyMap (and exercise them) or move the unsupported-but-documented ones out of the executable spec.config into prose/okapi-only notes so the declared set matches ApplyMap exactly.

Add core/formats/icml/schema.go exposing the Config keys for CLI/UI/reference docs (L2 Config+Schema criterion, weight 'high'); ideally with a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys (would also catch the spec/ApplyMap drift above).
formats.top_risk: Malformed/truncated ICML is silently mis-extracted, not rejected: the reader's XML decode loops bail out with a bare `break` on any decoder error (reader.go ~L146 and ~L288) and never emit a PartResult.Error, so a corrupt or non-XML file yields a partial-or-empty Block stream that is indistinguishable from a valid empty document — and the byte-exact skeleton writer would then emit a truncated/garbage document. There is no malformed_test to catch this.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/idml/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated ZIP, corrupt central directory, valid-ZIP/broken-Story_*.xml, and non-XML garbage stories — run with -race. The ~1300-line parseStory path (reader.go:241+) is the largest unguarded parse surface today.

Config/Schema (high weight): add core/formats/idml/schema.go implementing SchemaProvider so the 6 Config flags (extractMasterSpreads/extractNotes/skipDiscretionaryHyphens/extractHiddenLayers/extractHiddenPasteboardItems/extractHyperlinkTextSourcesInline) expose a JSON Schema for CLI/UI/reference; the nativedocs sidecar currently records 'no JSON Schema'. Ideally pair with a schema_test asserting Schema keys == ApplyMap keys.

Parity hygiene for L3 (lower priority, already mostly there): give the two discretionary-hyphen expected_fail entries an explicit structured divergence_kind (e.g. fixture-artefact) rather than prose-only justification, so the xfail audit is machine-checkable (0 divergence_kind fields today).
formats.top_risk: No malformed_test means panic-safety on broken IDML is unproven: a truncated/corrupt ZIP central directory or a malformed/half-written Story_*.xml could panic inside the large parseStory XML walker (reader.go:241+) or scanHiddenStories pre-scan rather than surfacing a clean PartResult.Error, and nothing runs the package under -race. The reader correctly errors on a non-ZIP and a nil doc, but mid-parse XML corruption and structural ZIP damage are the untested failure modes most likely to take down a streaming pipeline.

blocking_gaps: Malformed/robustness (high weight, the sole strict L2 blocker): add core/formats/json/malformed_test.go asserting a clean PartResult.Error with assert.NotPanics on truncated input, garbage/binary bytes, deeply-pathological nesting, and nil doc, run under -race. Today there is no malformed_test.go and zero NotPanics in the package; the two invalid-JSON tests only check Error-on-channel, never wrap reads in NotPanics.
formats.top_risk: Unproven panic-safety on adversarial input. The reader hand-rolls a lenient JSON5-ish scanner (scanner.go, ~13KB) and the writer reconstructs by walking the original token stream (writer.go reconstructFromOriginal/writeTokenValue/writeTokenObject), with compiled-regex caches and recursive descent — yet no test feeds truncated, binary, or deeply-nested garbage with NotPanics/-race. A malformed input that panics (e.g. an unbalanced token stream during writer reconstruction, or an index walk off the end) would crash the streaming pipeline mid-flow rather than surface a clean Error on the channel."

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/garbage/binary/deeply-nested input through Open+Read and assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel, run with -race. This is the single L1->L2 (and rubric) blocker; the reader does AST-walk + byte-offset/skeleton-gap arithmetic (skelEmitGap, baseOffset) + HTML subfilter tokenization that is never exercised against adversarial input. Files: core/formats/markdown/{reader.go,malformed_test.go (new)}.

Make spec.yaml config keys 1:1 with ApplyMap: spec declares 6 keys but ApplyMap accepts translateBlockQuotes/translateHTMLBlocks/useCodeFinder/codeFinderRules too (config.go:81-131 vs spec.yaml). Add the missing keys with okapi_param/spec_refs or document why they are spec-omitted.
Promote the non-skeleton TestRoundTrip from assert.Contains to byte/semantic equality (reader_test.go:278) so the fallback writer path proves fidelity, not just substring presence.
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness test: the reader performs a full goldmark AST walk with per-node byte-offset/skeleton-gap arithmetic (skelEmitGap, baseOffset slicing) and an HTML-subfilter tokenizer, none of which is exercised against truncated, garbage, or adversarially-nested input — a malformed document could panic with an index/slice-out-of-range inside the Read goroutine instead of returning a clean PartResult.Error, and nothing in the test suite would catch it.

blocking_gaps: Config -> complete (L4 schema-drift dimension): add schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys exactly equal the keys ApplyMap accepts (i.e. markdown.Config's parameter set) so the two cannot drift; today only html/json have this. Cite core/formats/mdx/schema.go vs the markdown.Config keys validated in config.go ApplyMap.

Writer/Reader edge-case matrix (L4 fidelity): add fixtures + byte-faithful round-trip assertions for non-UTF-8 encodings, a UTF-8 BOM, CRLF line endings, and an all-Unicode body. Every current testdata/ and testdata/corpus/ file is UTF-8/LF prose, so the byte-faithful guarantee under encoding/BOM/CRLF variation is unproven. Cite core/formats/mdx/testdata/ + roundtrip_test.go.

Malformed robustness under -race (L4 hardening): run the existing malformed + corpus suites with the race detector in CI; the reader spawns a goroutine per delegated markdown span via NewSkeletonStore, so a race run would harden the opaque-fallback path. Cite core/formats/mdx/reader.go emitMarkdownProse sub-store creation.
formats.top_risk: Byte-faithfulness is only proven for UTF-8 / LF / no-BOM inputs. The reader does io.ReadAll then byte-slices the body and copies opaque regions verbatim, while delegated markdown spans are re-encoded via RenderRunsWithData; there is no encoding/BOM/CRLF fixture in testdata. A CRLF MDX file, a UTF-8 BOM, or a non-UTF-8 source could break the byte-exact round-trip (or shift opaque-region byte offsets) without any test catching it — the format's central acceptance bar (byte-faithful untranslated round-trip) is therefore unverified outside the UTF-8/LF happy path."

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/messageformat/malformed_test.go that feeds truncated pickers, unbalanced braces, lone closing braces, garbage/binary bytes, and mid-UTF8 truncation, asserting a clean error (from Open today) AND assert.NotPanics, run under -race. This is the single L1->L2 blocker the maturity report names.

Config/Schema (high weight): add core/formats/messageformat/schema.go exposing the (currently empty) parameter surface for CLI/UI/reference docs; ideally a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys (both empty) so the format participates in the schema-drift contract.

Writer round-trip fidelity (critical weight, latent): make the NON-skeleton writer path either reconstruct the picker structure or document/guard that picker patterns require a skeleton store; extend TestRoundTrip beyond 'Hello world' to assert plural/select equality without a skeleton store, or assert the lossy path is intentionally limited.
formats.top_risk: The non-skeleton writer path (writer.go writeBlock) is lossy for the format's defining constructs: it emits each extracted block's text joined by newlines, so a {count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}} pattern read WITHOUT a skeleton store is reconstructed as two separate lines, silently destroying the ICU picker structure. Byte-exact round-trip is proven only when a SkeletonStore is attached; any pipeline that drives messageformat without one corrupts output without erroring. TestRoundTrip only exercises 'Hello world', so this corruption is untested.

blocking_gaps: Malformed/robustness (high weight): add core/formats/mif/malformed_test.go asserting require.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated/garbage/unbalanced-brace and binary-junk MIF input, run with -race. This is an L2 requirement and the dashboard's named top gap; currently only nil-doc and empty-string are covered.

Config+Schema (high weight): add core/formats/mif/schema.go so Config implements format.SchemaProvider (it currently does not, so registerSchemaAndDecoder registers no schema for mif), exposing the 12 ApplyMap keys for CLI/UI/reference docs. L2 requires schema.go present.
schema_test (toward closing the Config gap fully): once schema.go exists, add a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys to prevent drift (only html/json have this today).
formats.top_risk: Tracked #558/#509 round-trip instability: when a <Marker> or <Char Tab> sits BETWEEN two <String> fragments inside one <Para>, the per-inline-code Block split model and the per-<String>-run skeleton-ref machine (findStringPositions) disagree on how to route text back into <String> slots, so strict read->write->re-read is not event-stable. The corpus suite handles two real fixtures (okapi-Test01.mif, okapi-TestMarkers.mif) by skipping strict equality (only extraction + no-panic asserted). This means marker/tab-interleaved paragraphs can silently mis-route translated text into the wrong slot on real FrameMaker documents — and there is no malformed_test guarding the historical findStringPositions index-out-of-range crash on arbitrary (non-corpus) input.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/mo/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + clean handling on a nil Part, a non-Block Part, an empty/garbage Block stream, and that the stub reader's Open() returns an error for nil/garbage docs; run with -race.

Corpus + invariants (medium/high, harvest L2-substitute): add okapi_skip_test.go documenting why there is no Okapi MO counterpart, an invariants_test.go (e.g. output always parses under gotext, header entry always present, keys sorted, deterministic), and a corpus_test.go over a real .po->Blocks->.mo fixture rather than only synthetic blocks.

Writer fidelity (critical for L2->L3-class quality): route text through RenderRunsWithData (or document why plain SourceText/TargetText flattening is spec-justified for a binary catalog), and add msgid_plural/msgstr[n] handling so plural gettext catalogs are representable.
formats.top_risk: The MO writer flattens inline runs by using block.SourceText()/TargetText() instead of RenderRunsWithData, and has no plural (msgid_plural/msgstr[n]) support. Blocks carrying inline codes (e.g. from the HTML/JSON readers) or gettext plural forms will silently lose their placeholders/plural variants in the compiled catalog with no error or test catching it — a correctness data-loss risk that the current semantic-equality tests (single-run blocks only) do not exercise.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (rubric weight: high) asserting NotPanics + a clean Error/graceful tolerance on truncated, garbage-binary, lone-CR, and unterminated-<mrk> input, run with -race. Currently absent; this is the primary L1->L2 blocker. Note: scanRawLines (core/formats/mosestext/reader.go:351) swallows io.ReadAll errors, so a failing reader test would also surface there.

Add schema.go (rubric weight: high) exposing the useCodeFinder/codeFinderRules config surface for CLI/UI/reference docs; Config in core/formats/mosestext/config.go currently has ApplyMap but no Schema() method. Required for the L2 floor ('schema.go present').
formats.top_risk: scanRawLines (core/formats/mosestext/reader.go:351) discards the io.ReadAll error: `if err != nil || len(data) == 0 { return nil }`. A mid-stream read failure (e.g. a broken/partial reader) is silently treated as an empty document -- the reader emits an empty LayerStart/LayerEnd with no error on the channel, so a truncated or I/O-failing input produces silent data loss rather than a surfaced PartResult.Error. There is no malformed_test to catch this, making it both the top correctness risk and the reason the missing malformed_test is load-bearing rather than cosmetic.

blocking_gaps: Config/Schema (high): add core/formats/odf/schema.go (DataFormatConfig schema for CLI/UI/reference) covering translateNotes + translateHiddenContent — currently absent; this is an explicit L2-floor requirement and the Config dimension is capped at partial without it.

Malformed (high): add core/formats/odf/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated ZIP, valid ZIP with corrupt/non-XML content.xml, missing content.xml, and garbage bytes; run with -race. Existing TestReadInvalidZip/TestReadNilDocument lack NotPanics and don't cover truncation/corrupt-XML or the writer reparse decode path.
formats.top_risk: The writer's reparse fallback (replaceContent) reindexes blocks by SOURCE TEXT (blockByText[b.SourceText()]) and applies a target only when the trimmed element text matches verbatim — so two paragraphs with identical source text collapse to one entry and a translation can be applied to the wrong/duplicate occurrence, while leading/trailing whitespace differences silently skip replacement. Combined with the untested malformed path (no NotPanics coverage on corrupt content.xml feeding the xml.Decoder in replaceContent), this is the highest correctness/robustness exposure; the skeleton path (block-id keyed) avoids the collision but the reparse path is a real, untested fallback."

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/openxml/malformed_test.go feeding a truncated/garbage ZIP, a valid ZIP with corrupt word/document.xml, and a non-OOXML ZIP — assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel, run with -race. Exercises the existing reader.go L118 'not a valid ZIP archive' path that is currently untested.

Spec (high weight): bring spec.yaml keys to 1:1 with config.go ApplyMap (10 documented vs 34 cases) so the SEED spec covers every applyable key with okapi_param + spec_refs, satisfying the L2 'keys 1:1 with ApplyMap' bar that the L3 level technically depends on.
Schema (high weight, L4): add a schema_test.go asserting schema.go Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys to close schema drift (only html/json have this today).
formats.top_risk: Robustness on hostile input is unproven: there is no malformed_test, so the reader's handling of truncated/corrupt ZIP or malformed word XML — across a hand-written XML state machine, ZIP decompression, and the field/fldChar splice helpers (pullLeadingFldCharEndIntoPrevParagraph et al.) operating on raw []byte — is never exercised for panic-safety. A crafted or corrupt .docx could panic (index/slice OOB in the byte-splicing or DML/SML parsers) rather than surfacing a clean PartResult.Error, which is a real DoS/crash surface for a format that processes untrusted user uploads.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/paraplaintext/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error / empty-but-safe output on truncated, garbage, and invalid-encoding (e.g. lone \xff, embedded NUL) input, run with -race. This is the strict L1->L2 cap.

Config/Schema (high): add core/formats/paraplaintext/schema.go (even an empty/no-params schema) so the format participates in CLI/UI/reference schema wiring; ideally a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys.

Writer (critical, correctness): route the non-skeleton writeBlock/blockText through RenderRunsWithData (target-else-source) instead of raw SourceText()/TargetText(), and promote TestRoundTrip from Contains() to byte/semantic equality so the fidelity claim is actually proven on the non-skeleton path.
formats.top_risk: The non-skeleton writer path (writeBlock/blockText) renders via block.SourceText()/TargetText() instead of RenderRunsWithData, so a block carrying inline Run structure with embedded non-translatable Data (e.g. injected by an upstream tool) would be silently flattened or dropped — and TestRoundTrip only asserts Contains() substrings, so the regression would pass undetected. The byte-exact guarantees only hold on the skeleton path.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight, L1->L2 blocker): add core/formats/pdf/malformed_test.go feeding truncated/garbage bytes (e.g. '%PDF-1.0\nstream\n...no endstream', unbalanced '(', odd-length hex, non-FlateDecode-but-claimed) and a non-PDF blob, asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error or zero blocks; run with -race. Currently absent.

Config/Schema (high weight, L2 floor): add core/formats/pdf/schema.go exposing the (empty) config surface for CLI/UI/reference generation; ideally a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys.

Spec 1:1 (high weight, L2 spec contract): reconcile spec.yaml's 6 top-level config keys with ApplyMap, which recognizes none. Either implement the params natively (indentThreshold/spacingTolerance/preserveWhitespace/lineSeparator/paragraphSeparator/useCodeFinder) so ApplyMap accepts them, or mark them bridge-only at the key level so spec.go's documented 'each key 1:1 with ApplyMap' holds — today only the single config-bearing example is bridge_only, not the key list.
formats.top_risk: Unbounded/byte-level parsing of attacker- or producer-controlled PDF bytes with zero malformed-input test coverage. extractStreams does a header lookback (data[headerStart-lookback:headerStart]) and scanLiteralString/scanHexString walk raw bytes; while they appear bounds-guarded, a truncated or adversarial PDF (missing endstream, unbalanced parens, claimed-FlateDecode-but-corrupt, multi-GB stream) has no test proving NotPanics + clean Error, and io.ReadAll of the whole document is unbounded — a malformed/huge PDF could panic or OOM in a streaming pipeline that is supposed to surface errors on the channel.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (HIGH weight): add core/formats/phpcontent/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + clean result on truncated PHP, unterminated single/double-quoted strings, unterminated heredoc/nowdoc (the lexer currently swallows the rest of file as string body), and non-PHP garbage; run with -race. This is the sole criterion capping the format at L1.
Config schema_test (HIGH weight, needed for L4 not L2): no schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys == ApplyMap keys; only html/json have one — schema can drift silently.
formats.top_risk: Untested malformed/robustness path: the lexer's heredoc/nowdoc and quoted-string parsers fall back to 'return what we have, advance to EOF' on a missing terminator, so an unterminated string or heredoc label silently consumes the entire remainder of the file into one Block instead of erroring — and nothing asserts NotPanics or a clean result on truncated/garbage input. Secondary: the non-skeleton writer re-quotes from phpQuoteType metadata and escapeDoubleQuoted only escapes a stray '"', relying on the reader having preserved escapes verbatim, with no test proving non-skeleton output re-parses to the same extraction (output tests use only assert.Contains)."

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/plaintext/malformed_test.go asserting a clean PartResult.Error AND testify NotPanics on truncated/binary/invalid-byte input, run with -race. This is the only requirement separating plaintext from L2 — every other L2 (and most L3) artifact (spec.yaml+spec_test, parity_spec_test, schema.go, reference wiring) already exists.

Corpus (medium, for the L2->L3 ceiling): promote synthetic-only testdata to include at least one upstream/real fixture (or spec.yaml input_file: okapi:...) so stable counts + byte-exact round-trip run over non-authored content.
formats.top_risk: Untested malformed/binary input. The reader does io.ReadAll then coreenc.ToUTF8 on the whole buffer; if a caller feeds a large or non-text/binary .txt (it is the generic fallback format for unstructured files), the transcode/error path and memory behavior are entirely unexercised — no test asserts NotPanics or a clean channel Error on garbage/truncated/non-UTF input, so a panic or unbounded read could surface only in production.

blocking_gaps: Malformed: add core/formats/po/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error (run with -race) on truncated input, unterminated quotes (e.g. 'msgid "unclosed'), garbage bytes, lone msgstr without msgid, bad msgstr[N] indices, and nil doc. This is the strict L2 entry requirement ('a malformed_test asserts clean errors without panic') and is the single capping gap below L2.
formats.top_risk: Unverified robustness on malformed/adversarial input. The reader's parseEntries/readContentSkeleton parse a free-form text grammar (unquotePO, Sscanf on msgstr[N] bracket parsing, continuation-line state machine, UTF-16/charset transcoding on Open) with zero malformed-input tests beyond a nil-doc check. A truncated or quote-unbalanced .po could silently drop entries, misattribute continuation lines, or panic, and nothing in the suite would catch it — and the skeleton writer's raw-msgstr verbatim path would then faithfully reproduce corrupted skeleton.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/properties/malformed_test.go (L1->L2 blocker, critical/high weight): feed truncated \u escapes, lone trailing backslash continuation at EOF, NUL/garbage bytes, and bare-CR pathological lines; assert require.NotPanics and that Read produces no panic, run under -race. This is the strictest unmet criterion capping the level at L1.
Add a schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys == Config ApplyMap keys (only html/json have one today) — required for full Config credit and an L4 prerequisite.

Converge the numbered_placeholders pure default-diff: add a bridge_config disabling the code-finder so {n} placeholders match native, removing it from expected_fail (cli/parity + core/formats/properties/spec.yaml).
formats.top_risk: No robustness/panic-safety coverage at all (no malformed_test, zero NotPanics assertions). The reader's hand-rolled byte-level scanners — decodeValueEscapes (slices s[i+2:i+6] for \u), parseProperty, rawValueText and rawKeyPrefix do raw index arithmetic on untrusted bytes; a truncated \u escape near EOF or a pathological continuation/separator layout could index out of range and panic the whole streaming pipeline goroutine on real-world malformed .properties files. This is untested.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/regex/malformed_test.go (high/critical weight, L2): assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on an uncompilable pattern (e.g. rule pattern '('), truncated/garbage bytes, and confirm nil-doc Open error; run under -race. The reader already routes compile failures to emitError, so this is a test-only gap but it is currently the strictest unmet L2 criterion.

Add core/formats/regex/schema.go (high weight, L2): a DataFormatConfig schema exposing rules/escapeType/escapeChar for CLI/UI/reference docs. The L2 floor literally requires 'schema.go present'; absence is a second L2 blocker independent of malformed_test.
Add a schema_test.go asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys (only html/json have this today) - needed once schema.go exists and for the eventual L4 schema-drift guard.
formats.top_risk: Robustness is entirely unproven for the one input that is uniquely fragile for THIS format: a user-supplied regular expression. An uncompilable or pathological (catastrophic-backtracking-prone, though RE2 mitigates) pattern, or garbage bytes, has no test asserting it yields a clean channel Error without panicking. The reader's emitError path looks correct on inspection, but with no malformed_test.go (and no -race run) there is no guard against a regression that turns a bad rule pattern into a panic in the extraction goroutine - which would crash the whole streaming pipeline rather than surfacing as a per-document error."

blocking_gaps: Encoding/BOM/CRLF/unicode edge-case matrix (Writer + Corpus, critical/high weight, L4): the whole reader/writer pipeline assumes UTF-8 (io.ReadAll -> string -> byte tokenizer, no encoding detection/transcode). No fixture exercises a UTF-16 .resw, a non-UTF-8 .resx, or CRLF/all-unicode inputs. Add a fixture matrix in roundtrip_test.go/corpus proving byte-faithful round-trip and correct value-splice under those encodings. core/formats/resx/{reader.go:83,writer.go:101}

schema_test.go (Config+Schema, high weight, L4): no test asserts Schema() Properties keys == Config.ApplyMap accepted keys, so adding/renaming a config key silently drifts the CLI/UI schema. Add core/formats/resx/schema_test.go asserting the two sets are equal (mirrors html/json). core/formats/resx/schema.go vs config.go:59-79

Zero native-bug xfails / tracked faithful-class divergences (L4): trivially satisfied today (no parity, no xfails) but L4 also wants fresh contract/maturity dashboards regenerated; confirm the format-maturity dashboard snapshot is regenerated post-change.
formats.top_risk: Encoding-unaware pipeline: reader.go does io.ReadAll then string()+byte tokenizer with no encoding detection, and writer.go re-tokenizes those same UTF-8-assumed bytes. A UTF-16/BOM-prefixed .resw (a normal .NET satellite-resource encoding) is byte-scanned as if UTF-8 — decodeEntities, placeholder lifting, and encodeText all operate on mis-decoded text, and any translated <value> is spliced back as UTF-8 into a UTF-16 document, corrupting it. The verbatim-splice design hides this for untouched files (round-trip stays byte-equal) but breaks the moment a value changes, and nothing in the test matrix covers non-UTF-8 input.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/rtf/malformed_test.go: feed truncated RTF, unbalanced braces, raw garbage, and a NUL/binary blob; assert the reader emits a clean PartResult.Error (or zero blocks) with require.NotPanics, run under -race. This is the single L1->L2 blocker (highest weight).

Add core/formats/rtf/schema.go with the format's DataFormatConfig schema (currently only the reflective registerSchemaAndDecoder path exists) so Config+Schema reaches 'complete' for L2; ideally a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys.

Fix the spec.yaml expected_fail hygiene: the native TestSpec logs 'assertions now pass — remove the expected_fail tag' for all 11 examples. Either set divergence_kind on each xfail (a feature-scope divergence, e.g. okapi-feature-scope, NOT default-diff/native-bug) and confirm via make parity-sandbox that the bridge actually diverges, or drop expected_fail where native+bridge agree. Without divergence_kind on every xfail, Parity cannot reach L3.
formats.top_risk: The non-skeleton writer path is lossy: writeBlock emits block.SourceText()/TargetText() through escapeRTF instead of RenderRunsWithData, and writeHeader hardcodes '{\rtf1\ansi\deff0\uc1' — so any reconstruction without a populated SkeletonStore drops all original RTF structure, formatting groups, and inline runs and rebuilds a minimal document. Byte/semantic fidelity is only ever proven over the 218-byte simple.rtf via the skeleton path; the 37KB upstream fixtures are read-only-tested. A merge that loses the skeleton (or a real-world doc whose structure the skeleton diffing mis-maps) would silently corrupt the document.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (HIGH weight, robustness): feed truncated/garbage/binary input and assert NotPanics plus a clean PartResult.Error on the channel; run with -race. Currently only nil-doc is covered (reader_test.go:91). This is the single most-cited L1->L2 blocker in docs/internals/format-maturity.md.

Add schema.go (HIGH weight, config+schema): L2 floor requires schema.go for the empty Config. Trivial here (no parameters) but absent (no Schema() method in core/formats/splicedlines/). Ideally also a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys.
formats.top_risk: The reader strips a trailing backslash from non-terminal continuation lines but only re-adds the splicer via skeleton/property bookkeeping, and the non-skeleton writer reconstructs continuations from naive strings.Split(text, "\n") -- so if AI/MT translation introduces or removes newlines in a multi-line block (or the writer runs without a skeleton store, as plain pipelines do), the backslash-continuation structure is silently miswritten or lost, and there is NO test asserting byte/semantic equality for the non-skeleton writer path (TestRoundTrip only uses assert.Contains). Round-trip byte fidelity is proven only when a SkeletonStore is wired in; the default writer path is unverified for fidelity.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/srt/malformed_test.go: feed truncated cues, missing/garbled timecode lines, sequence-then-EOF, non-numeric sequence, binary garbage, and nil doc; assert a clean PartResult.Error (or documented graceful degradation) and require.NotPanics, run under -race. This is the single L1->L2 blocker per the rubric's L2 floor (highest weight: 'high').

Reconcile the parity story: either correct spec.yaml's 'format: okf_subrip' to a native-only marker (and add okapi_skip_test.go documenting that .srt maps to okf_regex+fprm, not a dedicated filter) or formally mark Parity/Spec as na. The current state advertises a non-existent okapi id in both spec.yaml and the nativedocs sidecar.

Fix the nativedocs sidecar factual error (scripts/gen-refs/nativedocs/formats/srt.yaml line: 'Mirrors Okapi's SubRip filter (okf_subrip)') — there is no okf_subrip; describe it as a native SubRip reader.
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness coverage at all (no malformed_test.go, zero NotPanics assertions). The reader's hand-rolled 3-state line parser (reader.go readContentSkeleton/parseEntries) silently accepts any input — a non-timecode second line is blindly stored as the timecode, a sequence-then-EOF mid-cue is dropped, an unterminated cue is best-effort flushed — so malformed SubRip never errors and never panics, but also never signals a problem; corruption is swallowed and emitted back out. This is untested in any direction.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/tex/malformed_test.go asserting assert.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error (or graceful EOF) on truncated/garbage/binary/unterminated-environment input, plus Open(nil)/Open(nil-reader) rejection, run under -race. This is the single L1->L2 blocker per the dashboard.

Config+Schema (high weight): add core/formats/tex/schema.go exposing the (empty, paramless) okf_tex schema for CLI/UI/reference docs; L2 floor requires schema.go present even for a zero-param filter. Ideally pair with a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys (empty set).
formats.top_risk: Robustness is unproven on adversarial input: no malformed_test exists, and the reader does ad-hoc recovery over hand-rolled brace/command scanning with raw slices like p.source[cmdStartPos:p.pos]. The only robustness guards (TestRunawayBraces/TestRunawayCurly) assert NotEmpty rather than NotPanics, and nothing runs under -race, so a truncated/unbalanced .tex could panic or mis-slice instead of yielding a clean PartResult.Error.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/tmx/malformed_test.go asserting clean PartResult.Error + assert.NotPanics on truncated/garbage/non-XML/empty/nil-reader input, run with -race. Currently TestInvalidXml/TestOpenInvalidContent/TestOpenEmptyContent (reader_test.go:1210,1960,1966) do `_ = err` and prove nothing.

Spec (high weight): make spec.yaml config keys 1:1 with config.go ApplyMap — either drop/relocate the unsupported segType/consolidateDpSkeleton/propValueSep/inlineCodes keys or wire them into ApplyMap, and add the accepted useCodeFinder/codeFinderRules keys so the spec matches the struct.

Parity (high weight): set a non-default-diff, non-native-bug divergence_kind (e.g. okapi-bug) on each of the 5 expected_fail entries in spec.yaml, and remove/repair the dangling parity-annotations.yaml entries that point at non-existent code_fail.tmx / code_id_difference.tmx fixtures. Run make parity-sandbox + go test -tags parity -run TestParityTmxSpec to confirm green.
formats.top_risk: Robustness is entirely unproven on malformed input: the three malformed-class tests (TestInvalidXml, TestOpenInvalidContent, TestOpenEmptyContent) call the reader then discard the result with `_ = err`, asserting neither a clean error nor non-panic. With decoder.Strict=false plus offset arithmetic in the skeleton path (segEndPos = InputOffset()-len('</seg>'), reader.go:366) and rawText slicing by byte offsets, truncated/garbage TMX could silently drop content or slice out of range without any test catching a regression. A real malformed_test.go with NotPanics + clean-Error assertions (run with -race) is the single most important missing guard."

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/transtable/malformed_test.go that asserts NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated input, binary garbage, a header-only-no-rows file, a row with a bad crumb, and a doc that EOFs mid-line; run it under -race. This is the single highest-weight (high) L1->L2 blocker and the one cited in the maturity report for transtable.

Add core/formats/transtable/schema.go publishing the DataFormatConfig schema (the one allowSegments boolean key) for CLI/UI/reference docs — schema.go presence is a hard L2-floor requirement and is currently absent (high weight).

Promote at least one round-trip test to assert byte/semantic EQUALITY rather than assert.Contains: the skeleton-store path already buffers header + blank-line bytes to make a source-only document byte-exact, so a TestByteFaithful asserting full output == input (for the unmodified source-only case) is achievable today and would harden the critical Writer dimension (high weight, also needed toward L4).
formats.top_risk: Byte-faithful round-trip is engineered but unproven: the reader meticulously buffers verbatim header and whitespace-line bytes into the skeleton store (reader.go:152,252) specifically to enable byte-exact reconstruction, yet every round-trip test asserts only assert.Contains on substrings and the skeleton tests explicitly disclaim byte-equality. A silent regression in skeleton replay, line-ending preservation (splitLineEnd handles \n/\r\n/\r but no test round-trips CRLF/CR), or the always-quote/always-emit-target-column writer policy (writer.go:217-226 emits a third cell whenever a target locale is set, even for source-only rows) could corrupt structure on merge without any test failing.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/ts/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated/garbage/non-XML input and nil doc, run with -race. Reader uses decoder.Strict=false so this is the real exposure; this is the single L1->L2 blocker.

Config+Schema (high weight): add core/formats/ts/schema.go (DataFormatConfig schema for CLI/UI/reference) so the L2 'schema.go present' criterion is met; ideally a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys.

Spec quality (high weight, for solid L2): reconcile spec.yaml config keys with ApplyMap — either make ApplyMap accept (and the reader honor) decodeByteValues/escapeGT/inlineCodes, or mark them clearly bridge-only so 'keys 1:1 with ApplyMap' holds.
formats.top_risk: Reader runs encoding/xml with decoder.Strict=false (+ HTMLAutoClose/HTMLEntity), so malformed/truncated TS is silently parsed leniently rather than surfaced as a clean PartResult.Error — and nothing tests this. A corrupt input can yield a partial/mis-structured block stream with no error and no panic-coverage, risking silent data loss on real-world broken files.

blocking_gaps:
Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/ttml/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated/garbage XML (e.g. unterminated

, binary noise, missing ), run with -race. This is the single criterion capping ttml at L1 -> L2.

Reader (critical, for true completeness even though not the level cap): inline /
should be preserved as Ph/PcOpen/PcClose runs with verbatim Data rather than flattened/dropped; dropping attributes is lossy and prevents byte-exact round-trip whenever a caption contains inline markup (the skeleton path side-steps this only because it copies raw bytes verbatim and never edits inline-markup'd captions).

Parity (high, for L3): replace the prose expected_fail with a structured non-default-diff divergence_kind, and verify the head-to-head run (make parity-sandbox + cd cli && go test -tags parity -run TestParityTtmlSpec ./parity/formats/) is green; confirm no pure default-diff xfails remain.
formats.top_risk:
The non-skeleton writer path (writeFromSkeleton, writer.go:146-245) re-discovers

elements by string-offset surgery (strings.Index(segment,">") / LastIndex(segment,"

")) and aligns blocks to pElements purely by index. The reader FILTERS OUT empty

captions when emitting blocks, but writeFromSkeleton's pElements list includes ALL

(including empties). So any document with an empty

before a non-empty one desyncs the block->pElement mapping, silently writing the wrong translation into the wrong caption (or dropping/duplicating text) on the no-skeleton path. The skeleton-store path is correct and byte-exact, but the fallback used whenever SetSkeletonStore is not called is fragile and untested for the empty-caption case.

blocking_gaps: Malformed: add core/formats/ttx/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated XML, garbage bytes, an unclosed <Tu>/<Tuv>, and invalid/odd-length UTF-16 (the BOM/encoding path in reader.go readContent is wholly untested for failure) — run with -race. This is an explicit L2-floor requirement and the single highest-weight gap.

Config+Schema: add core/formats/ttx/schema.go implementing format.SchemaProvider (segmentMode enum 0/1/2, escapeGT bool) so registerSchemaAndDecoder actually registers a schema for ttx; ideally a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys. L2 floor requires schema.go.

Spec hygiene (needed before parity can converge): rewrite spec.yaml inputs to the canonical no-<Seg> TTX shape that the reader and reader_test actually use and that Okapi parses; the current <Seg>-wrapped inputs misdescribe both native behavior and the format.
formats.top_risk: The spec.yaml/parity contract is built on a fictitious <Seg> content-wrapper that neither real TTX nor Okapi's TTXFilter uses (TTXFilter.java reads text directly inside <Tuv>). Consequently all 12 parity expected_fail entries are stale (runner logs 'assertions now pass'), carry no divergence_kind, and are mis-attributed to a non-existent 'bridge bug #486' — so the format has zero genuine head-to-head parity coverage while appearing green. Worse, the inline-code handling that the annotations wave off as 'cosmetic' is a real correctness risk: the reader FOLDS <ut>/<df> inline markup into plain text, and on a non-skeleton write that text is re-emitted as escaped literals (e.g. 'Text in <b>bold</b>'), silently corrupting inline structure on any round-trip that does not go through the skeleton store."

blocking_gaps: Add schema.go implementing SchemaProvider for the txml Config (declaring allowEmptyOutputTarget) so CLI/UI/reference docs get a real JSON Schema; the nativedocs sidecar currently states there is none. (Config dimension, high weight — required for L2.)

Add malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated/garbage/unbalanced TXML (e.g. <txml> with unterminated <segment>, non-XML bytes, half a <ut>), run with -race. (Malformed dimension, high weight — required for L2.)
formats.top_risk: Robustness on malformed input is unverified. The reader uses encoding/xml with decoder.Strict=false and reads the whole document into memory, then walks tokens with several 'unexpected EOF' error returns — but no test feeds truncated/garbage/unbalanced TXML to prove the reader surfaces a clean PartResult.Error without panicking. A malformed <ut>/<segment>/<source> boundary, or a half-written file from an interrupted Wordfast export, could in principle slip past the lenient decoder and produce a mis-segmented Block or a panic in the offset-arithmetic splice logic (e.g. negative endPos guarded only by clamps) rather than a clean error.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error (run with -race) on truncated/garbage/binary input; nil-doc Open is already covered but malformed content is not. This is the single strictest unmet L2 requirement (rubric: Malformed = high weight, the systemic L1->L2 lever).

Add the harvest L2-substitute trio: okapi_skip_test.go (asserts no okf_versifiedtext bridge counterpart) + invariants_test.go + corpus_test.go. A native spec.yaml/spec_test exists but the rubric's harvest path is okapi_skip + invariants + corpus, not the native spec runner.

Add schema.go (+ ideally a schema_test asserting Schema() keys == ApplyMap keys) so the Config+Schema dimension is complete; config is empty today so the schema is trivial but still required for L2 'schema.go present'.
formats.top_risk: Writer renders block content via SourceText()/TargetText() instead of RenderRunsWithData, so it silently flattens any inline Ph/PcOpen/PcClose runs. It is harmless for the format as written (the line-based reader never emits inline codes), but it is a structural fidelity hole: if a tool ever inserts inline markup into a block before the writer, that markup is dropped with no error — and it is also the reason inline-code preservation cannot be claimed at the writer contract.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/vignette/schema.go (DataFormatConfig schema for the 6 params: partsNames/partsConfigurations/sourceId/localeId/monolingual/useCDATA) so CLI/UI/reference docs are schema-driven -- explicit L2 floor item and the maturity report's named top gap; the nativedocs sidecar currently says 'no JSON schema is published'.

Add core/formats/vignette/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated XML, unbalanced/garbage tags, and unterminated valueCLOB, run with -race -- L2 floor robustness requirement; absent today.

Make corpus actually exercise locally: TestRealWorldTest01 and TestDoubleExtraction both SKIP without okapi-testdata even though Test01.xml exists upstream -- wire/fetch the fixture (or vendor a trimmed copy) so byte/semantic round-trip on a real file is proven, not skipped.
formats.top_risk: Parity is illusory rather than head-to-head: 5 of 6 content spec examples are expected_fail because the okapi-bridge extracts 0 Blocks from every packageBody input (including the real Test01.xml), so the cross-implementation contract is effectively native-only with no Okapi-side oracle on extraction/ordering -- and none of the 6 expected_fail entries carry a divergence_kind, so the divergences are undocumented in structured form. The native reader's correctness on bilingual SOURCE_ID pairing and the POSIX target-locale gate (the most error-prone logic, reader.go emitBlocks) is therefore validated only against hand-written fixtures, never against the upstream filter's actual output."

blocking_gaps: Malformed/robustness (high weight, L2 blocker): add core/formats/vtt/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + clean PartResult.Error on truncated input, missing/garbage WEBVTT header, binary bytes, and lone-CR/control chars; run with -race. Nothing currently exercises any non-well-formed input path in reader.go/readContentSkeleton.

Spec keys not 1:1 with ApplyMap (high weight, L2 blocker): reconcile core/formats/vtt/spec.yaml (8 config keys) with config.go ApplyMap (4 keys). Either implement+accept discardCues/cueSettings/keepTimecodes/timeFormat in Config, or drop them from spec.config. As written, those 4 keys are rejected by ApplyMapViaJSON's DisallowUnknownFields — the spec advertises config the format cannot accept.
Schema (high weight): add core/formats/vtt/schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys == Config struct json tags (only html/json have this today).
formats.top_risk: The spec.yaml advertises 8 configuration parameters (discardCues, cueSettings, keepTimecodes, timeFormat, plus the 4 native), but (a) the 4 non-native keys are silently REJECTED by ApplyMap's DisallowUnknownFields, and (b) even the 4 native keys (maxCharsPerLine/maxLinesPerCaption/cjkCharsPerLine/splitWords) are dead — never read by reader.go or writer.go, so they do nothing. A user (or the okf_vtt transform path) who sets any line-wrapping option gets either an error or a silent no-op with no caption splitting, directly contradicting both the published schema and the reference docs. Combined with zero malformed-input testing, malformed/oversized VTT plus unsupported config is the most likely correctness/robustness failure in production.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight, the named L2 blocker): add core/formats/wiki/malformed_test.go feeding truncated/garbage/binary/non-UTF-8 input to Reader.Open+Read, asserting assert.NotPanics and a clean PartResult.Error (no partial-block corruption), run under -race. Currently absent entirely.

Parity (high weight): actually execute and green the bridge head-to-head — `make parity-sandbox` then `cd cli && go test -tags parity -run TestParityWikiSpec ./parity/formats/`; reconcile the documented native (.wiki/text/x-wiki) vs bridge (.txt/text/x-wiki-txt) ext/MIME divergence so the ParityRunner dispatches the bridge correctly; record any divergence as an attributed non-default-diff expected_fail.

Corpus (medium weight): add core/formats/wiki/corpus_test.go that round-trips the upstream okapi:okapi/filters/wiki/src/test/resources/dokuwiki.txt fixture byte-exact and asserts stable block counts, rather than relying on 3 synthetic testdata snippets.
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness coverage at all: the hand-written line/state-machine reader (readMediaWiki/readDokuWiki, header-delimiter slicing, infobox/table-cell carving, inline-code tokenizer) is exercised only on well-formed snippets. Truncated headers (unbalanced '='), unterminated <file>/<code> blocks, malformed {{image|…}} or [[link|…]] spans, or non-UTF-8/CRLF input could panic (e.g. slice/index out of range in the delimiter or run-splitting logic) or silently corrupt the skeleton, and nothing — not even a -race NotPanics test — guards against it.

blocking_gaps: Add an edge-case fidelity matrix proving byte-faithful round-trip across non-UTF-8/BOM/CRLF inputs and alternate JSON escaping conventions (\/ -escaped slashes, \uXXXX-escaped non-ASCII) — all current fixtures (5 synthetic + 3 corpus) are UTF-8/LF, so the bespoke scanner.go/rewrite.go + encodeJSONString round-trip is unproven against the escaping forms real Xcode/third-party emitters produce (L4 edge-case-matrix requirement).

Add schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys == ApplyMap accepted keys (extractStale, markTranslatedState) to lock schema/config from drift — only html/json have this today; it is the named L4 item for xcstrings.

Add a benchmark / perf guard for the full-document re-tokenizing rewriter (rewrite.go walks every token of the original on every write), since byte-faithfulness is O(document) per write and is the format's hot path (L4 bench/perf for the rewrite engine).
formats.top_risk: Byte-faithfulness rests entirely on the bespoke scanner.go/rewrite.go token walker plus encodeJSONString re-encoding, validated only against UTF-8/LF fixtures. A real catalog using a different-but-valid escaping (\/ slashes, \uXXXX non-ASCII), a UTF-8 BOM, or CRLF would make the re-emitted value/state strings diverge from the originals (encodeJSONString applies its own escaping rules), silently breaking the byte-faithful guarantee even for UNCHANGED leaves — and no edge-case fixture exercises this.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (high weight): add core/formats/xliff/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated XML, garbage bytes, unclosed <trans-unit>/<source>, and a nil RawDocument to Open — run with -race. This is the single L3→L4 blocker per the rubric dimension-6 table (xliff listed: 'malformed_test.go (L4)').

Corpus (medium): replace synthetic-only testdata (just simple.xlf) with an encoding/CRLF/BOM/all-Unicode edge-case fixture matrix and at least one real-world / okapi-upstream .xlf, proving byte-exact round-trip across them (the L3→L4 'edge-case matrix + real-world corpus' requirement).
Config schema_test (medium): add a schema_test.go asserting Schema() field set == ApplyMap recognized keys, to close schema-drift risk (currently only html/json have this; L4 requires it).
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness test exists, so the byte-exact skeleton-store round-trip path is unproven against adversarial input. The writer's fidelity depends entirely on the SkeletonStore reconstruction; a truncated/garbage XLIFF, an unclosed element, or a charset-transcode failure could panic or silently corrupt the skeleton (e.g. a partially-emitted Block paired with a skeleton entry) and there is zero test — under -race or otherwise — that the read→write cycle degrades cleanly rather than panicking or producing structurally-broken XML on malformed input.

blocking_gaps: Writer (critical): resolve or formally reclassify the RED TestRoundTrip_ByteEqualUntouched in core/formats/xliff2/roundtrip_test.go — 21 upstream fixtures fail byte-equality (#560). Either fix the DOM writer to preserve untouched segments byte-exact, or convert these to an attributed faithful-class divergence (extend notExpectedByteEqual with spec+Okapi-grounded reasons) so the package's `go test` is green; a checked-in failing fidelity test blocks a clean L1->L2.

Malformed (high): add core/formats/xliff2/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated/garbage XML, mismatched/unclosed tags, and bad inline-code IDs; run with -race. This is the explicit L2 entry criterion and is entirely absent.

Parity (high, for L3 readiness): verify cli/parity/formats/xliff2_spec_test.go passes head-to-head under -tags parity + make parity-sandbox, and add a parity-annotations.yaml attributing any divergence (inline-tag canonicalization, original-data) with a non-default-diff divergence_kind citing the XLIFF 2 spec and Okapi XLIFF2Filter.
formats.top_risk: The DOM-based writer is not byte-faithful on unmodified input (TestRoundTrip_ByteEqualUntouched RED across 21 real upstream fixtures, #560): merging an AI/MT pass back into a customer's XLIFF 2 silently rewrites inline-tag forms, attribute order/namespaces, and original-data on segments nobody edited, producing noisy diffs and risking semantic drift in inline codes — exactly the round-trip fidelity an XLIFF exchange format must guarantee.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (HIGH): add core/formats/xml/malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on truncated tags, unclosed elements, byte-garbage, invalid UTF-8, and a non-XML payload; run with -race. This is the L2-row requirement and the dashboard's named top gap.

Config (HIGH): make ApplyMap reject unknown top-level keys and unknown parser.* keys (config.go:504-505 and 500-502 currently silently ignore) — or formally document the forward-compat policy; then add a schema_test asserting schema.go Schema() field set == ApplyMap recognized keys (only html/json have schema_test today).

Parity verification (HIGH, toward L3 confidence): run make parity-sandbox + cd cli && go test -tags parity -run TestParityXmlstreamSpec ./parity/formats/ head-to-head against the okf_xmlstream bridge daemon to confirm green; the wiring is complete but live equivalence was not executed in this audit.
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness test: truncated, byte-garbage, or non-XML input is never fed through Open→Read, so there is no proof the reader surfaces a clean PartResult.Error without panicking. The reader does emit parse errors on the channel (reader.go:1360, :201) and rejects nil docs in Open, but the encoding/xml token loop and the rule-driven sax handler (reader.go:817, :995, :1068) are untested against adversarial input — a panic on malformed bytes would crash any pipeline stage consuming the channel. This is the single concrete robustness hole and the dashboard-named L-blocker.

blocking_gaps: Malformed (critical, L1→L2 blocker): add core/formats/yaml/malformed_test.go feeding truncated/garbage/non-YAML bytes (e.g. unterminated quote, bad indentation, raw binary) through reader.Open+Read, asserting a clean PartResult.Error with require.NotPanics, run under -race. Today only Open(nil) and unknown-config-key are covered.

Spec keys not 1:1 with ApplyMap (high, L2 spec criterion): spec.yaml config uses bridge names (extractAll, includeIsolatedStrings, useAsName, …) with zero overlap to the native ApplyMap keys (extractNonStrings, useCodeFinder, keyPathPatterns, codeFinderRules, subfilter), and no example exercises a native config key. Either alias the bridge names into Config.ApplyMap (as the spec header promises) or rewrite spec.config to the native keys and add config-driven examples (extractNonStrings, keyPathPatterns, useCodeFinder) with assertions.
Config schema_test (high): add a schema_test.go asserting Schema() property keys == Config.ApplyMap accepted keys to lock out drift (only html/json have this today).
formats.top_risk: Unproven crash-safety on malformed input. The reader streams parse errors onto the channel (good), but there is no test that drives truncated/garbage/invalid-YAML bytes through Open+Read to prove it returns a clean Error instead of panicking — and the writer has hand-rolled byte-range scanning (scanQuotedEnd, block-scalar indent/CR capture in reader.go) that is exactly the kind of index arithmetic that panics on adversarial input. Combined with the spec.yaml config keys being entirely bridge names with zero native ApplyMap coverage (no config-driven example ever runs against the native reader), the format's robustness and config behavior are effectively untested outside the happy path.
